_about: Hand-built labeled question set for the Michigan FAP (SNAP) manual corpus. Each in-corpus question names the gold BEM/BAM section(s) that support the answer, used to score retrieval (context precision/recall, hit-rate, MRR) and citation accuracy. Out-of-corpus questions test grounded refusal: the answer is real but lives outside this scoped corpus, so the system should decline rather than invent. Gold sections were assigned from the parsed section content.

questions.question: If people live together but buy and prepare their food separately, are they in the same food assistance group?
questions.reference_answer: Generally each group is people who live together and purchase and prepare food together; some who buy and prepare separately can be separate groups, with exceptions (e.g., spouses, certain parents and children).

questions.question: Must spouses who live together be in the same food assistance group?
questions.reference_answer: Yes. Spouses living together must be in the same food assistance group even if they buy and prepare food separately.

questions.question: Are children under 22 who live with their parents included in the parents' food assistance group?
questions.reference_answer: Children under 22 living with a parent must be in the parent's group, even if they purchase and prepare food separately.

questions.question: Does categorical eligibility waive the asset test for food assistance?
questions.reference_answer: Yes. Categorically eligible food assistance groups automatically meet the asset test, with limited exceptions.

questions.question: What makes a food assistance group categorically eligible?
questions.reference_answer: Receipt of certain benefits/services (e.g., FIP, SSI, or a TANF-funded service such as the information brochure under broad-based categorical eligibility) confers categorical eligibility.

questions.question: Can a group lose categorical eligibility because of lottery or gambling winnings?
questions.reference_answer: Yes. A substantial lottery or gambling winning (a single winning of $4,500 or more) can cause a group to lose categorical eligibility and face the asset test.

questions.question: What counts as a countable asset for food assistance?
questions.reference_answer: Countable assets include cash, money in checking and savings accounts, and many investments; some assets are excluded.

questions.question: How are vehicles treated as an asset?
questions.reference_answer: Vehicle treatment follows the asset rules in BEM 400; certain vehicles are excluded or partially counted.

questions.question: Is the home a person lives in a countable asset?
questions.reference_answer: The homestead the person lives in is generally an excluded asset.

questions.question: What is the difference between earned and unearned income?
questions.reference_answer: Earned income is received from employment or self-employment for work performed; unearned income is income received without working, such as benefits or support.

questions.question: What kinds of income are excluded from food assistance budgeting?
questions.reference_answer: Certain income types are excluded from the budget; the income overview and the unearned-income section list excluded income types.

questions.question: How is income from employment counted for food assistance?
questions.reference_answer: Gross earned income from employment (wages, salaries, tips, commissions) is counted per BEM 501.

questions.question: Are tips and commissions counted as earned income?
questions.reference_answer: Yes, tips and commissions are part of earned income from employment.

questions.question: How is self-employment income calculated for food assistance?
questions.reference_answer: Self-employment income is gross receipts minus allowable business expenses of producing the income.

questions.question: What expenses can be deducted from self-employment income?
questions.reference_answer: Allowable expenses of producing the self-employment income are deducted; some expenses are not allowable.

questions.question: Is a person who provides child care in their own home self-employed?
questions.reference_answer: Yes. A person providing child care in their own home is considered self-employed; if care is in the child's home they are an employee.

questions.question: Is rental income treated as self-employment income?
questions.reference_answer: Rental income is sometimes self-employment and sometimes unearned; the system determines the type and countable portion.

questions.question: Is child support received counted as income for food assistance?
questions.reference_answer: Child support received is generally counted as unearned income.

questions.question: Is Supplemental Security Income (SSI) counted as unearned income?
questions.reference_answer: SSI is counted as unearned income for food assistance.

questions.question: Are food assistance benefits themselves counted as income?
questions.reference_answer: No. FAP issuances are not entered as income.

questions.question: Is Social Security (RSDI) counted as unearned income?
questions.reference_answer: RSDI/Social Security benefits are counted as unearned income.

questions.question: How is income prorated when a group member is disqualified?
questions.reference_answer: When a member is disqualified (e.g., for SSN, citizenship, or time limit), the income is prorated and the remainder of the computation is unchanged.

questions.question: How is gross income determined in the food assistance budget?
questions.reference_answer: Gross income is the total countable earned and unearned income before deductions, computed per FAP income budgeting rules.

questions.question: What shelter expenses are allowable for the excess shelter deduction?
questions.reference_answer: Allowable shelter expenses include rent or mortgage and related housing costs; home equity loans are allowable.

questions.question: Can legally obligated child support paid out be deducted?
questions.reference_answer: Yes, legally obligated child support paid to a non-household member is an allowable deduction.

questions.question: Are dependent care costs an allowable expense for food assistance?
questions.reference_answer: Yes, dependent care costs needed for work, training, or education are allowable.

questions.question: Whose medical expenses can be deducted for food assistance?
questions.reference_answer: Out-of-pocket medical expenses for senior, disabled, or disabled-veteran members are deductible above a threshold.

questions.question: What worksheet is used to compute the food assistance budget?
questions.reference_answer: The DHS-2242 Food Assistance Worksheet (the automated budget in Bridges) is used.

questions.question: Are cents dropped when computing earned income for the budget?
questions.reference_answer: Cents are included per person, then dropped before totaling the group's gross earned income.

questions.question: How is the food assistance benefit amount computed?
questions.reference_answer: The benefit is computed from the income calculation on the worksheet, applying deductions to reach net income and the allotment.

questions.question: What is a collateral contact in the verification process?
questions.reference_answer: A collateral contact is a verbal or written confirmation of information from a person or organization outside the household.

questions.question: Who is responsible for obtaining verification of eligibility factors?
questions.reference_answer: The client is primarily responsible for providing verification, with agency assistance in certain circumstances.

questions.question: How much time does a client have to provide requested verification?
questions.reference_answer: Clients are given a standard number of days (a verification due date) to provide requested documentation.

questions.question: What sources can be used to verify information for a case?
questions.reference_answer: Documents, collateral contacts, and electronic data sources can be used to verify information.

questions.question: Is there a cap on the excess shelter deduction, and who is exempt from it?
questions.reference_answer: The excess shelter deduction is capped, except groups with a senior or disabled member, for whom it is uncapped.

questions.question: How do you decide whether income is employment or self-employment?
questions.reference_answer: Indicators of self-employment include setting one's own hours, providing one's own tools, and being responsible for the service and methods.

questions.question: What is the maximum monthly cash assistance payment under the Family Independence Program?
questions.reference_answer: Out of corpus: cash assistance payment standards are not in the scoped FAP sections.

questions.question: How do I apply for unemployment insurance benefits in Michigan?
questions.reference_answer: Out of corpus: unemployment insurance is administered separately and is not in this manual subset.

questions.question: How is the Medicaid spend-down amount calculated for a family of three?
questions.reference_answer: Out of corpus: Medicaid budgeting is not in the scoped FAP sections.

questions.question: What is the federal SSI benefit rate for 2026?
questions.reference_answer: Out of corpus: SSI federal payment amounts are not stated in these sections.

questions.question: What is the income limit for the Child Development and Care subsidy?
questions.reference_answer: Out of corpus: the child care subsidy program is not in this manual subset.

questions.question: How do I request a hearing to appeal a benefits denial?
questions.reference_answer: Out of corpus: hearings procedure (BAM 600) is not in this scoped corpus, which includes only BAM 130 from the administrative manual.

questions.question: What is the LIHEAP home heating energy assistance benefit amount?
questions.reference_answer: Out of corpus: energy assistance benefits are not in the scoped FAP sections.

questions.question: What is the maximum food assistance allotment in dollars for a household of four?
questions.reference_answer: Out of corpus: the dollar allotment tables (reference tables) are not contained in these policy sections.
